title: Add Ready condition and ServerList status to Memcached CR
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#3](https://github.com/C5C3/memcached-operator/issues/3)

Add a `Ready` condition type to `computeConditions()` in `internal/controller/status.go` with reasons `MemcachedReady`/`MemcachedNotReady` — true only when `readyReplicas == desiredReplicas && desiredReplicas > 0`. Add `ServerList []string` field to `MemcachedStatus` in both `api/v1beta1/memcached_types.go` and `api/v1alpha1/memcached_types.go`, wired through `memcached_conversion.go`. Compute `serverList` as `["<cr-name>.<namespace>:<port>"]` (service DNS) in `reconcileStatus()` when Ready=True, nil otherwise. Regenerate CRD manifests via `make manifests`. Extend `TestComputeConditions` table in `status_test.go` with Ready assertions across all scenarios. Add integration tests in `memcached_status_reconcile_test.go` for Ready condition and serverList population. Add ServerList to conversion round-trip coverage in `memcached_conversion_test.go`. Update `docs/reference/crd-reference.md` with new status fields.

**Rationale:** The forge repo's Chainsaw E2E tests assert `status.conditions[?type=='Ready'].status == 'True'` on Memcached CRs, matching the contract established by MariaDB and ClusterIssuer CRs. Without a Ready condition, these tests timeout after 5 minutes and fail CI (https://github.com/C5C3/forge/actions/runs/23404038405). The `SimulateMemcachedReady` simulator in forge confirms the expected contract: Ready condition + readyReplicas + serverList. This blocks all forge infrastructure E2E test suites.

**Affected Areas:**
- api/v1beta1/memcached_types.go
- api/v1alpha1/memcached_types.go
- api/v1alpha1/memcached_conversion.go
- api/v1alpha1/memcached_conversion_test.go
- internal/controller/status.go
- internal/controller/status_test.go
- internal/controller/memcached_status_reconcile_test.go
- config/crd/bases/memcached.c5c3.io_memcacheds.yaml
- docs/reference/crd-reference.md

stories.title: Forge E2E tests can assert Ready condition on Memcached CR
stories.want: a Ready condition on the Memcached CR that is True when all desired replicas are ready
stories.so_that: Chainsaw E2E tests stop timing out and the forge infrastructure test suite passes
criteria: status.conditions contains a condition with type='Ready'
Ready=True only when readyReplicas == desiredReplicas AND desiredReplicas > 0
Ready=False when readyReplicas < desiredReplicas or desiredReplicas == 0
Ready condition has reason 'MemcachedReady' when True and 'MemcachedNotReady' when False
Ready condition has a non-empty human-readable message

stories.title: Consumer discovers Memcached server endpoint via serverList
stories.want: a serverList status field containing the service DNS entry when the instance is ready
stories.so_that: I can programmatically discover the Memcached endpoint without hardcoding DNS patterns
criteria: status.serverList contains ["<cr-name>.<namespace>:11211"] when Ready=True
status.serverList is nil/empty when Ready=False
serverList uses the headless Service DNS name and standard Memcached port 11211
serverList is updated on every reconciliation cycle alongside conditions

stories.title: v1alpha1 users see ServerList in status after conversion
stories.want: the ServerList field to be preserved during v1alpha1↔v1beta1 conversion
stories.so_that: deprecated API consumers see the same status fields as v1beta1 users
criteria: ServerList field exists in both v1alpha1 and v1beta1 MemcachedStatus
ConvertTo copies ServerList from v1alpha1 to v1beta1
ConvertFrom copies ServerList from v1beta1 to v1alpha1
Round-trip conversion preserves ServerList with no data loss

stories.title: Existing conditions remain unchanged after adding Ready
stories.want: the existing Available, Progressing, and Degraded conditions to be unaffected
stories.so_that: adding the Ready condition does not break existing monitoring or tooling
criteria: Available, Progressing, Degraded conditions retain their existing semantics exactly
computeConditions now returns 4 conditions instead of 3
All existing unit test assertions for Available/Progressing/Degraded continue to pass
All existing integration test assertions continue to pass

stories.title: CRD reference documents new status fields
stories.want: the CRD reference to document the Ready condition and serverList field
stories.so_that: I understand the semantics of the new status fields without reading source code
criteria: MemcachedStatus table includes serverList field with type and description
Status Conditions table includes Ready condition with description
Ready condition semantics (readyReplicas == desiredReplicas && desiredReplicas > 0) are documented
serverList computation logic (service DNS when Ready=True, nil otherwise) is documented

requirements.description: computeConditions() SHALL return a Ready condition that is True only when readyReplicas == desiredReplicas AND desiredReplicas > 0
requirements.rationale: Matches the forge Chainsaw E2E test contract: status.conditions[?type=='Ready'].status == 'True'

scenarios.name: All replicas ready
scenarios.when: readyReplicas == desiredReplicas (e.g., 3/3) and desiredReplicas > 0
scenarios.then: Ready condition status is True with reason MemcachedReady

scenarios.name: Partial replicas ready
scenarios.when: readyReplicas < desiredReplicas (e.g., 1/3)
scenarios.then: Ready condition status is False with reason MemcachedNotReady
and_then: message indicates how many replicas are ready vs desired

scenarios.name: No replicas ready
scenarios.when: readyReplicas == 0 and desiredReplicas > 0
scenarios.then: Ready condition status is False with reason MemcachedNotReady

requirements.description: Ready condition SHALL be False when desiredReplicas == 0, even though no replicas are 'missing'
requirements.rationale: Zero-replica instances are not serving traffic and should not report Ready=True

scenarios.name: Zero desired replicas
scenarios.when: spec.replicas == 0 and deployment has 0 ready, 0 total
scenarios.then: Ready condition status is False with reason MemcachedNotReady

scenarios.name: Nil replicas defaults to 1, fully ready
scenarios.when: spec.replicas is nil (defaults to 1) and 1 replica is ready
scenarios.then: Ready condition status is True with reason MemcachedReady

requirements.description: Ready condition SHALL be False when deployment is nil (not yet created)
requirements.rationale: Before the deployment exists, readyReplicas is 0 so the instance cannot be ready

scenarios.name: Nil deployment with positive desired
scenarios.when: deployment has not been created yet and spec.replicas > 0
scenarios.then: Ready condition is False with reason MemcachedNotReady

scenarios.name: Nil deployment with HPA active
scenarios.when: HPA is active but deployment is nil
scenarios.then: Ready condition is False, falls back to spec.replicas as desired count

requirements.description: reconcileStatus() SHALL populate serverList as ["<name>.<namespace>:11211"] when Ready=True, nil otherwise
requirements.rationale: The forge SimulateMemcachedReady simulator expects serverList populated alongside Ready=True

scenarios.name: Ready instance has serverList
scenarios.when: Ready condition is True after reconciliation
scenarios.then: status.serverList contains exactly ["<cr-name>.<namespace>:11211"]
and_then: port is the standard Memcached port 11211

scenarios.name: Not-ready instance has nil serverList
scenarios.when: Ready condition is False after reconciliation
scenarios.then: status.serverList is nil

scenarios.name: ServerList updates on transition
scenarios.when: instance transitions from Ready=True to Ready=False (e.g., scaling up)
scenarios.then: serverList is cleared to nil on the next reconciliation

requirements.description: ServerList field SHALL be present in both v1alpha1 and v1beta1 MemcachedStatus and preserved during conversion
requirements.rationale: Both API versions must expose the same status contract for downstream consumers

scenarios.name: v1alpha1 to v1beta1 preserves ServerList
scenarios.when: a v1alpha1 Memcached with serverList=["mc.ns:11211"] is converted to v1beta1
scenarios.then: the v1beta1 status.serverList equals ["mc.ns:11211"]

scenarios.name: v1beta1 to v1alpha1 preserves ServerList
scenarios.when: a v1beta1 Memcached with serverList=["mc.ns:11211"] is converted to v1alpha1
scenarios.then: the v1alpha1 status.serverList equals ["mc.ns:11211"]

scenarios.name: Round-trip preserves nil ServerList
scenarios.when: a minimal Memcached with nil serverList is round-tripped
scenarios.then: serverList remains nil after round-trip

requirements.description: computeConditions() SHALL return exactly 4 conditions (Available, Progressing, Degraded, Ready) in all scenarios
requirements.rationale: Consumers expect a stable set of condition types; the addition must not break the existing 3

scenarios.name: Four conditions returned
scenarios.when: computeConditions is called with any valid input combination
scenarios.then: the returned slice contains exactly 4 conditions with types Available, Progressing, Degraded, Ready
and_then: all conditions have non-empty Message, Reason, and valid LastTransitionTime

scenarios.name: Existing conditions unchanged
scenarios.when: computeConditions is called with inputs matching existing test scenarios
scenarios.then: Available, Progressing, and Degraded have same status/reason as before the change

requirements.description: Ready condition SHALL use reason 'MemcachedReady' when True and 'MemcachedNotReady' when False
requirements.rationale: Distinct reason strings allow consumers to programmatically distinguish readiness states

scenarios.name: MemcachedReady reason when True
scenarios.when: Ready condition is True
scenarios.then: condition.Reason == 'MemcachedReady'

scenarios.name: MemcachedNotReady reason when False
scenarios.when: Ready condition is False
scenarios.then: condition.Reason == 'MemcachedNotReady'

requirements.description: Ready condition SHALL correctly handle HPA-managed replica counts
requirements.rationale: When HPA is active, desired replicas come from Deployment status — Ready must use the same source

scenarios.name: HPA active, all ready
scenarios.when: HPA active, dep.Status.Replicas=5, readyReplicas=5
scenarios.then: Ready=True with reason MemcachedReady

scenarios.name: HPA active, partially ready
scenarios.when: HPA active, dep.Status.Replicas=5, readyReplicas=3
scenarios.then: Ready=False with reason MemcachedNotReady

scenarios.name: HPA active, nil deployment
scenarios.when: HPA active but deployment nil
scenarios.then: Ready=False, falls back to spec.replicas

requirements.description: CRD manifest SHALL include serverList in the status schema after running make manifests
requirements.rationale: Generated CRD YAML must reflect the new field for API server validation

scenarios.name: serverList in generated CRD
scenarios.when: make manifests is run after adding ServerList to both API version types
scenarios.then: config/crd/bases/memcached.c5c3.io_memcacheds.yaml contains serverList as items:string array in status for both versions

requirements.description: docs/reference/crd-reference.md SHALL document the Ready condition and serverList field
requirements.rationale: Users need documentation to understand new status fields

scenarios.name: Ready condition in Status Conditions table
scenarios.when: user reads the Status Conditions section
scenarios.then: Ready condition is listed with True/False values and description matching REQ-001 semantics

scenarios.name: serverList in MemcachedStatus table
scenarios.when: user reads the MemcachedStatus section
scenarios.then: serverList is listed with type []string and description of service DNS format

tasks.title: Add ServerList field to v1beta1 and v1alpha1 MemcachedStatus + regenerate deepcopy (REQ-005, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Add `ServerList []string` field with json tag `serverList,omitempty` and kubebuilder +optional marker to MemcachedStatus in api/v1beta1/memcached_types.go (after ObservedGeneration, line ~325). Add the identical field to api/v1alpha1/memcached_types.go. Run `make generate` to regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go for both packages. Verify compilation with `go build ./...`.

tasks.title: Wire ServerList through v1alpha1↔v1beta1 conversion + update conversion tests (REQ-005)
tasks.description: In api/v1alpha1/memcached_conversion.go: add `dst.Status.ServerList = src.Status.ServerList` to both ConvertTo (line ~57) and ConvertFrom (line ~108). In api/v1alpha1/memcached_conversion_test.go: add `ServerList: []string{"full-mc.prod:11211"}` to the Status block of fullyPopulated() helper (line ~161). Add ServerList assertion to TestConvertTo_FullyPopulatedObject. The existing TestRoundTrip_FullyPopulated_NoDataLoss uses reflect.DeepEqual on Status so it will automatically cover the new field. Run `go test ./api/...` to verify.

tasks.title: Add Ready condition to computeConditions() + extend all unit tests with Ready assertions (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In internal/controller/status.go: (1) Add constants ConditionTypeReady='Ready', ConditionReasonReady='MemcachedReady', ConditionReasonNotReady='MemcachedNotReady'. (2) Change capacity from 3 to 4 on line 67. (3) After the Degraded condition block (line ~137), add Ready condition: ready := desiredReplicas > 0 && readyReplicas == desiredReplicas; set status/reason/message accordingly. Append to conditions slice. In internal/controller/status_test.go: (1) Add wantReady/readyReason fields to the TestComputeConditions table struct. (2) Add Ready assertions (wantReady + readyReason) to all 8 existing test cases. (3) Update TestComputeConditions_ReturnsThreeConditions → ReturnsFourConditions (expect 4, check for 'Ready' type). (4) Add Ready assertion to all HPA and SecretNotFound tests. (5) Verify ObservedGeneration test covers Ready. Run `go test ./internal/controller/ -run TestComputeConditions`.

tasks.title: Compute serverList in reconcileStatus() based on Ready condition (REQ-004)
tasks.description: In internal/controller/status.go reconcileStatus(): After setting conditions (line ~162) and before setting readyReplicas, check if the Ready condition is True by inspecting the freshly-set conditions via meta.FindStatusCondition. If Ready=True, set mc.Status.ServerList = []string{fmt.Sprintf("%s.%s:%d", mc.Name, mc.Namespace, PortMemcached)}. If Ready=False, set mc.Status.ServerList = nil. Import the PortMemcached constant from the same package (deployment.go). Update the logger.Info call to include serverList. Run `go build ./...` to verify compilation.

tasks.title: Regenerate CRD manifests via make manifests (REQ-009)
tasks.description: Run `make manifests` to regenerate config/crd/bases/memcached.c5c3.io_memcacheds.yaml. Verify the generated CRD contains serverList as type: array with items: type: string under status for both v1alpha1 and v1beta1 versions. Run `go build ./...` and `go vet ./...` to verify no issues.

tasks.title: Add integration tests for Ready condition and serverList in memcached_status_reconcile_test.go (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-004, REQ-006)
tasks.description: In internal/controller/memcached_status_reconcile_test.go: (1) In the 'initial reconciliation with default 1 replica' Context, add It block asserting Ready=False (envtest deployment has 0 ready replicas) and serverList is nil. (2) In 'scaled to zero replicas' Context, add It block asserting Ready=False and serverList is nil. (3) In 'all three conditions always present' Context, update to expect 4 conditions (HaveLen(4)) and assert 'Ready' key present. (4) Add It block verifying Ready condition has correct ObservedGeneration. (5) In 'initial reconciliation with explicit 3 replicas' Context, add Ready=False assertion. Run `make test` to verify all tests pass.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/crd-reference.md with Ready condition and serverList (REQ-010)
tasks.description: In docs/reference/crd-reference.md: (1) Add serverList row to the MemcachedStatus table (after observedGeneration): field=serverList, type=[]string, description='List of Memcached server endpoints in <name>.<namespace>:<port> format. Populated when Ready=True, nil otherwise.' (2) Add Ready row to the Status Conditions table: type=Ready, values=True/False, description='True when all desired replicas are ready (readyReplicas == desiredReplicas && desiredReplicas > 0)'. (3) Verify markdown renders correctly.

test_specifications.expected: After initial reconcileOnce, Ready condition is False since envtest deployment has 0 ready replicas

test_specifications.expected: status.serverList is nil/empty after initial reconciliation where Ready=False

review_criteria: All existing unit tests in status_test.go pass unchanged for Available/Progressing/Degraded conditions (backward compatibility)
All existing integration tests in memcached_status_reconcile_test.go pass (no regressions)
All existing conversion tests pass (round-trip fidelity preserved with ServerList)
Ready condition is True only when readyReplicas == desiredReplicas && desiredReplicas > 0 across all test scenarios
serverList is populated as ["<name>.<namespace>:11211"] only when Ready=True, nil otherwise
computeConditions returns exactly 4 conditions in all code paths
CRD manifest (make manifests) includes serverList field in both API versions
go vet ./... and golangci-lint pass with no new warnings
docs/reference/crd-reference.md accurately documents Ready condition semantics and serverList format
implementation_notes:
Architecture: computeConditions() is a pure function taking (mc, dep, missingSecrets, hpaActive) and returning conditions. The Ready condition uses the same desiredReplicas/readyReplicas variables already computed at the top of the function. The serverList computation happens in reconcileStatus() after conditions are set, checking the Ready condition status via meta.FindStatusCondition(). The service DNS format '<name>.<namespace>:<port>' uses the headless Service created by reconcileService(), which always has the same name as the CR. Port is PortMemcached (11211) from deployment.go.

Data Flow:
1. reconcileStatus() fetches Deployment → calls computeConditions() → sets conditions on mc.Status.Conditions
2. After conditions set, check Ready condition → if True, set mc.Status.ServerList = ["name.ns:11211"], else nil
3. Set readyReplicas, observedGeneration → r.Status().Update(ctx, mc)

Key patterns to follow:
- Condition constants: ConditionType* and ConditionReason* in status.go (lines 19-40)
- Condition construction: metav1.Condition{Type, Status, Reason, Message, LastTransitionTime, ObservedGeneration}
- Test table structure: TestComputeConditions has wantAvailable/availReason etc. — add wantReady/readyReason
- assertCondition helper: reuse for Ready assertions
- Conversion: direct field copy pattern — dst.Status.ServerList = src.Status.ServerList
- fullyPopulated() test helper: add ServerList to Status block for round-trip coverage

Pitfalls:
- capacity in make([]metav1.Condition, 0, 3) must change to 4
- TestComputeConditions_ReturnsThreeConditions must be updated to expect 4
- Integration test 'all three conditions always present' HaveLen(3) must become HaveLen(4)
- The PortMemcached constant is in deployment.go — same package, no import needed
- meta.FindStatusCondition works on mc.Status.Conditions after SetStatusCondition has been called
